description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly dark brown and gray surface, featuring subtle earthy undertones and weathered streaks. One side of the rock is covered with a patch of green moss or lichen, giving it a natural, aged appearance. The surface is rugged and uneven, with visible indentations and pitted textures, especially where the moss is present. The rock's form is asymmetrical, with one end appearing slightly more elevated and rounded, while the opposite side is flatter and broader. The coloration transitions smoothly from darker, mossy areas to lighter, exposed stone, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. Its overall look is consistent with rocks commonly found in landscaped urban parks or as decorative barriers along sidewalks.
description: A rugged, dark brown and gray rock with a patch of green moss and a pitted, weathered surface. Its irregular shape and natural textures make it suitable for urban landscaping or as a barrier.
description_view_0: This view highlights the smoother, broader side of the rock with minimal moss and visible weathered streaks.
description_view_1: The top surface is shown here, dominated by a dense patch of green moss and numerous pitted indentations.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the transition between the mossy, pitted area and the lighter, exposed stone, showing more surface detail.
description_view_3: The opposite side is mostly bare stone with subtle streaks and a rounded, less textured appearance.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or roadside barrier
